perturbation.original_text: Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee.
perturbation.changed_text: Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall not have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides that Bank of America has the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. The modified text removes the 'without paying any reinstatement fee,' creating uncertainty and contradicting the previous statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement. Bank of America may terminate Maintenance Services for convenience at any time in accordance with the Section entitled "Termination" of the Agreement. If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall not have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Bank of America had the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee. The changed text removes the "without paying any reinstatement fee" which directly contradicts the original agreement. Furthermore, the contradicted text specifies that Bank of America shall not have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services. This creates a direct conflict with the original allowance to reinstate, thus, an in-text contradiction exists, which adheres to the definition of "Omissions – In Text Contradiction", where essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The Software License and Maintenance will be provided at no charge.
perturbation.changed_text: The Software License and Maintenance will be provided with fees according to the Price List.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the Software License and Maintenance will be provided at no charge. The modified text contradicts the earlier statement by stating that the Software License and Maintenance will be provided with fees according to the Price List.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PAYMENT TERMS The Software License and Maintenance will be provided with fees according to the Price List.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Software License and Maintenance would be provided at no charge. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that it will be provided with fees according to the Price List. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as the two statements are mutually exclusive. The contradicted text reinforces the updated statement about fees. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be twenty- four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, including Bank of America holidays.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be defined in a later agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the Maintenance Period shall be twenty-four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, including Bank of America holidays. By stating that the Maintenance Period shall be defined in a later agreement creates an contradiction within the section
perturbation.contradicted_text: DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 120 days Notwithstanding anything set forth elsewhere in this Agreement (or below), the Maintenance Period shall be defined in a later agreement. 120 days
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defined the Maintenance Period as 'twenty-four (24) hours per day, seven (7) days per week, including Bank of America holidays.' The changed text states that the Maintenance Period 'shall be defined in a later agreement.' This creates a contradiction because the original definition is removed, and now the definition is uncertain, conflicting with the existing '120 days' specification. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language defining the maintenance period is removed, creating a conflict with the duration stated in the contradicted section. The 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition is also met because there are clauses in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
